1.premise: Santa can seem more real than people because culture outlives biology.

lines.text: been doing a lot of drugs and dwelling on my traumas.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And mushrooms showed me that my biggest trauma
lines.label: setup

lines.text: was learning that Santa Claus was not real.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: When you think about Santa, when you're on mushrooms,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: you realize they've been talking about Santa
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And they're going to keep talking about Santa
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Yet I'm only going to live for like 80 years.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So in a way, isn't Santa more real than me?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: If Santa's not real, and I'm less real than Santa,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: then why did it feel real when he molested me at the mall?
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Our traumas ground us in the physical reality.
lines.label: tag
